pi_items.source: Verified by reading setup_task.sh and install_gvsig.sh, which place the Natural Earth countries shapefile at this path.
Verified using geopandas to inspect the ne_110m_admin_0_countries.shp file; the attribute 'CONTINENT' exists and contains continent names.
Verified by checking verifier.py and export_result.sh which explicitly check for the shapefile at this output path.
Verified via python script. The source shapefile contains exactly 8 distinct 'CONTINENT' values, which falls within the expected range of 5 to 10. The verifier.py also hardcodes this range.
Verified by extracting distinct values from the CONTINENT column in the source shapefile. All of the required values are indeed present in the data.
Verified via O*NET Online database (code 17-1021.00 correctly corresponds to this title).
Verified via O*NET Online; the SOC 17-0000 family corresponds to Architecture and Engineering Occupations.

privileged_info_summary: The task operates on the public Natural Earth countries dataset (ne_110m_admin_0_countries.shp). The objective is to dissolve country boundaries by the 'CONTINENT' field and export the result to /home/ga/gvsig_exports/continents_dissolved.shp. Analysis of the source shapefile confirms it contains exactly 8 distinct continent values (including Africa, Asia, Europe, North America, South America, and Oceania), directly satisfying the expected range of 5 to 10 features upon successful completion of the dissolve operation. All metadata claims exactly match the data contents, standard occupation codes, and task instructions.
